title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Headaches
intro: Herbal teas for Headaches are a type of medicinal tea made from the leaves, flowers, or roots of plants, used to alleviate and soothe the symptoms of headaches. They can treat this ailment by providing natural relief from pain, inflammation, and stress, which are common causes of headaches. Examples of herbal teas that can help alleviate headaches include Feverfew, which contains compounds that prevent the release of pain-causing chemicals; Ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling; Peppermint, which has a cooling effect that can help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow; and Lavender, which has a calming effect that can help reduce stress and promote relaxation. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Chamomile, Willow Bark, and Rosemary can also help alleviate headaches due to their anti-inflammatory, pain-relieving, and stress-reducing properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their soothing and sedative properties, which help to alleviate stress and anxiety that often trigger these episodes. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovaleric acid, which have been shown to possess a calming effect on the nervous system and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from headaches. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Valeriana officinalis teas help to treat headaches by addressing the underlying causes of the ailment. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat headaches include reduced frequency and severity of episodes, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the reliance on over-the-counter pain medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and a cup of boiling water.
Place the dried root in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea slowly and as needed to help relieve headaches.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists", Valeriana officinalis teas may be potentially safe for headaches.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be effective in treating headaches, but it may also cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat headaches, as it may worsen conditions such as kidney or liver problems, and it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it before driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 30 minutes before headache.
Take 1 cup of tea every 4-6 hours as needed for headache relief.
Start with 250mg of dried Valeriana root per cup of tea.
Gradually increase to 500mg per cup of tea if needed, but not exceeding 1g.
Consult a doctor if headache persists or worsens after using Valeriana officinalis tea.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat headaches by reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The bioactive constituents of Foeniculum vulgare teas, including volatile oils such as anethole and limonene, contribute to their therapeutic effects by acting as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent. By consuming Foeniculum vulgare teas, individuals can benefit from reduced headache frequency and severity, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare (aniseed) in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat until it starts boiling.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Strain the mixture into a cup and let it cool before drinking. You can add honey if needed.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to treat headaches, but possible side effects include digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. When using Foeniculum vulgare teas to treat headaches, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed, and also to avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, as this may exacerbate digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed, up to 3 times daily.
Use Foeniculum vulgare tea for headaches as needed, but no longer than 7 days.
Consult a healthcare professional if headaches persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, help to alleviate headache symptoms by blocking pain pathways and reducing inflammation in the body. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can experience relief from headaches as the compounds in ginger work to calm and soothe the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing tension. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat headaches include its natural and non-habit-forming properties, making it a popular and effective herbal remedy for headache sufferers.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Measure 1/2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder.
Add the powder to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve in a cup. Drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of integrative medicine, Zingiber officinale teas for headaches may be effective in managing head pain by improving gastric function and reducing gastrointestinal disorders associated with headaches, as suggested by Persian medicine and supported by some contemporary scientific evidence.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective herbal remedy for headaches, but possible side effects may include nausea, stomach upset, and heartburn due to its warm and spicy properties. To safely use Zingiber officinale teas for headache relief, take it in moderation, as excessive consumption can exacerbate digestive issues, and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach to minimize stomach upset.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Zingiber officinale in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Start with a low dose of 1/2 teaspoon and gradually increase as needed.
Combine Zingiber officinale tea with ginger oil for added relief if needed.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for up to 3 consecutive days for headache relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, derived from the Lavender plant, have been used for centuries to treat headaches due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and relaxing properties. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, help to ease headache pain by reducing muscle tension, promoting relaxation, and inhibiting the release of pain-causing chemicals. By drinking Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals can experience relief from headaches as the herbal preparation helps to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat headaches include their non-addictive and non-pharmacological nature, making them a safe and natural alternative to conventional pain relief medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it's boiling.
Add the dried flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, as needed for headache relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating headaches, but it may also cause drowsiness and stomach upset in some individuals. To use this herbal preparation safely, individuals should start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious if they have sensitive stomachs or allergies.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Lavandula angustifolia tea leaves in a cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for headaches.
You can also add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
For severe headaches, consider consulting a healthcare professional for guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their soothing and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat headaches by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety and stress, and improving sleep quality, ultimately leading to a decrease in headache frequency and severity. The bioactive constituents, such as rosmarinic acid and borneol, possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to ease muscle tension and reduce pain. The benefits of Melissa officinalis teas in treating headaches include natural pain relief, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves per 8 oz cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea hot or cold, 2-3 times a day as needed to help with headaches.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Revista de neurologia", Melissa officinalis teas for headaches can be used as a treatment, with its effectiveness differing depending on the origin of the headache, as suggested by the 13th-century medical poem, De secretis mulierum, from the School of Salerno.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat headaches, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its potential interactions with certain medications. Precautions to take when using Melissa officinalis teas include avoiding excessive consumption, especially in pregnant or breastfeeding women, as it may stimulate the uterus and affect milk production.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Melissa officinalis tea as needed for headaches.
Start with 1/2 cup and adjust to 1 cup as needed, up to 3 times a day.
Use 5-7 grams of dried Melissa officinalis leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and discard the leaves.
Consult with a healthcare professional for chronic or severe headaches.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used for centuries as a natural remedy to treat headaches, particularly those caused by migraines and tension. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and inflammation, while also improving blood flow to the brain, which can contribute to the alleviation of headache symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to have a positive effect on the regulation of neurotransmitters, which can help to prevent headache attacks. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat headaches include their ability to provide fast and effective relief, as well as their potential to reduce the frequency and severity of headache episodes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy them at a health food store.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove until it reaches 212°F (100°C).
Pour the hot water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Ginkgo biloba leaves. Drink the tea slowly to help relieve headaches.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists", Ginkgo biloba teas for headaches have been identified as potentially safe for use in preventing or treating headaches.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Ginkgo biloba teas can be effective in treating headaches due to their ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation; however, possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals.

Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat headaches include being aware of the risk of bleeding due to its blood-thinning properties, especially when combined with other medications or if you have bleeding disorders; it's also essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed to minimize potential side effects, such as headaches or dizziness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup.
Gradually increase dosage to 1/2 teaspoon per cup if needed.
Drink tea when headache starts or before going to bed.
Consult a doctor for proper dosage and frequency if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been used to treat headaches due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat headaches by providing relief from pain and reducing inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate headache symptoms. Regular consumption of peppermint tea may also help to reduce migraine frequency and severity, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for those suffering from headaches.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey and have it ready for later.
Steep the Mentha x piperita leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves. Add the honey to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea as needed to help relieve headache symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Cephalalgia : an international journal of headache", Mentha x piperita teas for headaches have been found to produce a significant analgesic effect with a reduction in sensitivity to headache when combined with ethanol.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can provide relief from headaches due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, but potential side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. To safely use Mentha x piperita teas to treat headaches, one should be cautious when consuming large amounts, avoid using them during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be aware of potential interactions with blood thinners and other medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Mentha x piperita tea when headache starts.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain before consumption.
Drink 2-3 cups a day, or as needed for headaches.
Consult a doctor for long-term or severe headache treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas, also known as Passionflower tea, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat headaches by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which in turn alleviates the symptoms of tension headaches. The bioactive constituents of Passionflower tea, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, are responsible for its medicinal properties, which help to block pain pathways and reduce muscle spasms. Regular consumption of Passiflora incarnata teas can provide relief from headaches and promote overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 tablespoon of dried Passiflora incarnata leaves. You can buy them online or in health stores.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water. Pour it into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add the 1 tablespoon of Passiflora incarnata leaves to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea strainer. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea when it's still warm. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Central nervous system agents in medicinal chemistry, Passiflora incarnata teas for headaches may be similar in anxiolytic-like effects to the species P. sidaefolia, as both reduced anxious-like behavior in the Holeboard test.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be effective in treating headaches, but they may cause drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Passiflora incarnata teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it as needed, and also to avoid consuming them before driving or operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat headaches by reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and volatile oils, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium tea may provide benefits in reducing headache frequency and severity, promoting relaxation, and improving overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, or 2 teaspoons of dried herbs.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink the tea as needed to relieve headaches, up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Medicines (Basel, Switzerland), Achillea millefolium teas for headaches are not specifically mentioned in the provided study, as the study primarily focuses on reviewing Cherokee ethnobotanical literature and phytochemical investigations on Cherokee medicinal plants, but it does mention yarrow (Achillea millefolium) as one of several Cherokee medicinal plants still in use today as herbal medicines.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be an effective herbal remedy for headaches, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to be cautious when combining with other medications or substances that may interact with the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas, derived from the bark of the Cinchona tree, have been traditionally used to treat headaches, particularly those associated with malaria, due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis teas, such as quinine and other alkaloids, help to treat headaches by reducing fever, relieving pain, and inhibiting the growth of the malaria parasite. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat headaches include their ability to provide rapid relief, reduce the severity and frequency of attacks, and promote overall well-being. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis teas are a natural and non-addictive alternative to synthetic pain relievers, making them a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to headache management.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried bark to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the bark and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the bark.
Drink the tea slowly and enjoy, ideally 2-3 times a day for headache relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can cause gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and stomach cramps, in some individuals due to its high quinine content. Precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, as this may lead to dizziness, rapid heartbeat, and other cardiovascular issues, particularly in people with pre-existing heart conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of rosemary tea, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. By consuming rosemary tea, individuals can experience relief from headaches as it helps to relax the muscles, improve circulation, and reduce stress, ultimately providing a soothing effect on the body. The benefits of using rosemary tea to treat headaches include its natural and non-addictive properties, making it a popular alternative to over-the-counter pain medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea slowly and as needed for headache relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to History of medicine studies, Rosmarinus officinalis teas for headaches have been used since ancient times as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat headaches, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high concentration of volatile oils. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including starting with a low dose and gradually increasing it, as well as avoiding consumption before or after surgery, as it may increase the risk of bleeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including thujone, borneol, and camphor, help to reduce pain and inflammation, leading to relief from headaches. By relaxing muscles, reducing tension, and modulating neurotransmitters, Artemisia absinthium teas help to alleviate headache symptoms and promote overall well-being. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat headaches include its non-addictive nature, lack of side effects, and natural ability to provide relief without relying on pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot.
Pour the hot water over the Artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in a cup.
Let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups as needed for headache relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plants (Basel, Switzerland), Artemisia absinthium teas for headaches are not specifically mentioned in the study, however, a closely related species, Artemisia afra, was listed as one of the most popular plant species used in the management of headache, with 2 documented instances.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat headaches, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat headaches, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon tea, have been used for centuries to treat headaches due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, help to reduce pain and inflammation, thus providing relief from headaches. Cinnamomum verum teas work by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body and by improving blood circulation, which helps to alleviate headache symptoms. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat headaches include reduced frequency and severity of headaches, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinnamomum verum powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Cinnamomum verum powder to a cup.
Pour the boiling water over the powder.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and drink when it's cooled down to a comfortable temperature.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be used to help alleviate headaches due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, but its usage may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Cinnamomum verum teas in moderation, avoid mixing it with other medications, and be cautious when taking it during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to limited research on its effects on these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been widely used as a natural remedy to alleviate headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which help to soothe and relax the mind and body. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which possess analgesic and antispasmodic properties that help to reduce pain and tension associated with headaches. By consuming Matricaria chamomilla teas, individuals can experience relief from headache symptoms, including reduced frequency and severity of episodes, as well as improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat headaches include a natural and non-invasive approach, reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, and a potential boost to overall well-being and mental health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the flowers in a cup or teapot and add the boiling water.
Let the flowers steep for 5-7 minutes to release the tea's properties.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea hot or cold, depending on your preference, to help relieve headaches.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Matricaria chamomilla teas may be used as an acute treatment for headaches, as indicated by positive preliminary findings.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat headaches, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with ragweed allergies. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to use Matricaria chamomilla teas in moderation, avoid taking them before bedtime, and monitor your body's response to the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitex agnus-castus teas have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their potential to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce stress levels. This herbal preparation may help to alleviate headache symptoms by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system and modulating the body's response to pain. The bioactive constituents of Vitex agnus-castus teas, such as flavonoids, iridoid glycosides, and diterpenoids, are thought to contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body. Overall, the benefits of using Vitex agnus-castus teas to treat headaches include reduced frequency and severity of episodes, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves.
Put 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Add the dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for headache relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitex agnus-castus teas can cause a range of side effects, including dizziness, headaches, nausea, and stomach upset, which may exacerbate the symptoms being treated, and also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and hormonal therapies. To minimize potential risks, it's essential to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming these teas during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as well as if you have a history of hormone-related conditions or are taking medications that affect hormone levels.

preparations.herb_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis teas, derived from the roots of the Baikal skullcap plant, have been traditionally used to treat headaches, including migraines and tension headaches. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as baicalein and baicalin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the brain. These compounds also possess a sedative effect, which can help to relax tense muscles and improve sleep quality, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of headaches. By consuming Scutellaria baicalensis teas, individuals can benefit from its analgesic and anti-anxiety properties, leading to improved overall well-being and a reduction in headache-related symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of Scutellaria baicalensis dried roots.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey and have it ready for use.
Steep the Scutellaria baicalensis dried roots in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the roots. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea.
Drink the tea while it is still warm. Repeat as needed to help alleviate headaches.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Biochimica et biophysica acta, Scutellaria baicalensis teas for headaches may be beneficial due to the antioxidant properties of baicalein and baicalin, which can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Scutellaria baicalensis teas can be effective in treating headaches due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming Scutellaria baicalensis teas on an empty stomach, as this may exacerbate stomach upset.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper nigrum teas, also known as black pepper tea, have been used for centuries to treat headaches due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat headaches by reducing pain and inflammation, thus providing relief from symptoms such as migraine and tension headaches. The bioactive constituents of black pepper tea, including piperine and other bioactive compounds, help to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and block the transmission of pain signals, making it an effective remedy for headaches. The benefits of using black pepper tea to treat headaches include its ability to provide rapid relief, reduce the frequency and severity of headaches, and promote overall well-being without any side effects.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tsp of dried Piper nigrum (black pepper) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tsp of honey (optional) to a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Add the dried Piper nigrum to the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a different cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea slowly and enjoy the relief from your headache.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Frontiers in pharmacology, Piper nigrum teas for headaches is not mentioned in the provided study content, as the study discusses the neuroprotective potential of various Indian spices, including Piper nigrum, but does not specifically mention its use for headaches.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Piper nigrum teas can be used to treat headaches due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties; however, possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals.

When using Piper nigrum teas to treat headaches, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach to minimize the risk of stomach upset, and also to be cautious when combining it with other medications or herbal preparations.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been traditionally used to treat headaches, offering relief from migraines and tension headaches. The properties of this herbal preparation, such as its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with headaches. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, including avenanthramides and avenacosides, play a crucial role in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which in turn helps to treat headaches. The benefits of Avena sativa teas for treating headaches include reduced frequency and severity of headaches, improved sleep quality, and a sense of calm and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa root, 1 cup of water, and a saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil in the saucepan.
Reduce heat and add the dried Avena sativa root.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea slowly and repeat as needed for headache relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat headaches, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the presence of saponins and other compounds that can interact with the body's nervous system. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming them on an empty stomach, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery as they can cause drowsiness and impair coordination.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as citral, geraniol, and limonene that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches. The citral content in lemongrass tea is believed to have a sedative effect, which helps to alleviate headache symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Regular consumption of Cymbopogon citratus teas may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of headaches by promoting overall well-being and reducing stress levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea slowly and as needed to relieve headache symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study on Journal of ethnopharmacology, Cymbopogon citratus teas for headaches may provide relief due to its analgesic properties, as Cymbopogon plants have been traditionally used to treat ailments including headache and abdominal pain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used to treat headaches, but possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Glycyrrhizin, a key compound in Glycyrrhiza glabra, has been shown to help treat headaches by reducing the severity of migraine attacks and improving overall symptoms. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat headaches include reduced frequency and severity of attacks, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in reliance on over-the-counter pain medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. You can buy this at a health food store or online.
Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or kettle.
Add the 1 teaspoon of dried root to the boiling water. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea while it's hot. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, I couldn't find any information about Glycyrrhiza glabra teas being used for headaches. However, licorice (Glycyrrhiza glabra) is mentioned as possibly offsetting the pharmacological effect of spironolactone.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat headaches, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and increased blood pressure in some individuals due to its high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that can interact with the body's hormonal balance. To safely use this herbal preparation, it is recommended to consume it in moderation, avoid consuming it for extended periods, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or substances, such as potassium supplements or diuretics, to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
